description:
GemCraft: Frostborn Wrath is a hex-grid tower defense game with gem crafting.
Complete stages to receive field tokens that unlock further stages, all shuffled
into an Archipelago multiworld.

V0.0.5.description:
# v0.0.5 — Alpha Release

A big update over 0.0.4 covering achievements, drop icons, gem pouches, the wizard stash, DeathLink, the in-game UI, and a long list of bug fixes.

## What's new

### Achievements are now part of your run
- Every in-game achievement can now act as an Archipelago location check. Completing one sends an item to the multiworld.
- Added to in-game **Achievement Browser**. Lists every achievement on the current stage, filtered into groups, with a search bar.
- Tracker dots next to each entry show whether an achievement is in logic, collected, or excluded.
- Achievements you can't reasonably reach in randomizer logic are marked **untrackable** and skipped automatically.
- New yaml option **Achievement Effort** — Trivial / Minor / Major / Extreme decides how deep into the achievement list checks go.
- The pop-up that appears when you collect an achievement now shows its current logic status, even before opening the panel.

### Wizard Stash now locks behind keys
- Every stage's Wizard Stash starts **locked** and must be opened with a Stash Key delivered through Archipelago.
- Stash Keys come in several granularities — per-stage, per-tile, per-tier, or a single global master key, each with a progressive variant.
- Opening a stash counts as a check, adding 122 new locations beyond the regular stage clears.

### Gem Pouches now matter
- Gem Pouches are now items you receive from the multiworld. Until you have the right pouch, the matching gem colour can't be created.
- Pouch granularity is configurable — `off`, per-tile, per-tier, or `global`, each with a progressive sibling.
- A **Hollow Gem** starter mechanic gives you a plain colourless gem on your starter stage so you can play before your first real Gem Pouch arrives. In Frostborn mode the Hollow Gem also grants up to 3 free starter towers.

### More yaml options for shaping your run
- Field Token, Stash Key, and Gem Pouch granularities all have **progressive** variants — each copy unlocks the next slot in a predetermined order.
- Field Token Placement: any world, own world only, or other worlds only.
- Difficulty multipliers: enemy HP, armor, shield, monsters-per-wave, and an extra-wave-count option.
- Skillpoint Multiplier, XP Tome Bonus, Starting Wizard Level, Starting Overcrowd, Disable Endurance, Disable Trial.

### DeathLink got a major overhaul
- DeathLink is now properly driven by your yaml — turned on automatically at the start of every connect (you can still toggle it off mid-session).
- **Five** punishment modes to choose from:
  - **Gem Loss** — destroys a percentage of placed gems with their towers/traps rounded upwards.
  - **Wave Surge** — extra batches of enraged monsters drip in over ~15 seconds
  - **Instant Fail** — the current stage fails immediately
  - **Spawn Horde** *(new)* — 50–500 vanilla-strength monsters from the current wave arrive at once
  - **Spawn Special** *(new)* — a burst of Apparitions, Wraiths, Specters, Spires, or Wizard Hunters scaled to the current wave
- Configurable grace period at stage start and cooldown between successive punishments.
- DeathLinks you cause yourself are no longer echoed back to you, and an Instant Fail that kills your orb no longer bounces a fresh DeathLink to the player who sent it.
- The DeathLink received toast tells you how many seconds until it actually triggers.

### Drop icons completely redone
- In-game drop icons now show every Archipelago item that was on that location.
- Icons match the item type — XP tomes, field tokens, talismans, shadow cores, gem pouches, stash keys, skills, traits, achievements all have their own visual.
- Foreign-game items use a generic Archipelago icon with the real item and location names underneath.
- Item names use Archipelago's importance colours (progression / useful / trap / filler).
- Drop icons wait for the level outcome before showing instead of dribbling in one by one.

### Better messaging during play
- A **persistent scrollable message log** (toggle with `` ` ``) records every item you sent and received, colour-coded by importance.
- Outgoing toasts read **"Sent X to Player (Found at Location)"** with both the location and the receiving player's display name.
- Receive toasts colour-code item names the same way.
- Items the mod can't identify fall back to the name Archipelago provides instead of a numeric ID.

### Offline check collection
- A new **Offline Summary Window** shows every check you completed while disconnected. Reconnect and they get sent out, and the window keeps a record so nothing slips by.

### Field tooltips
- Hovering a stage shows whether it's in logic, what stage elements / monster types it contains, whether its stash is unlocked, and what requirements (skills, talismans, etc.) are needed to play it.

### Per-slot settings panel
- A new in-game panel summarises the configuration of the slot you're connected to — goal type, difficulty multipliers, granularities, DeathLink settings, and more.

### Universal Tracker support
- The apworld now plays nicely with Universal Tracker. Re-launches don't blow away your seed-specific options.
- Has been extensively tested but may stay result in mismatches.

### Standalone mode reinstated
- You can again launch the mod and play without ever connecting to Archipelago. The mod stays inert and the game plays as vanilla for that slot.

### Other in-game UI touches
- First-launch disclaimer screen.
- Main menu shows the mod version and apworld version.
- In-game changelog viewer.

## Bug fixes since 0.0.4

- DeathLink **not loading at all** from the yaml on connect — fixed.
- DeathLink firing the moment you connect without playing a level — fixed.
- Grace period timer now counts down during normal play, so a DeathLink arriving 5 seconds into a stage applies after the remaining grace, not a fresh full grace period.
- Skillpoint bundles being **deducted on every return to the main menu** instead of just once — fixed.
- Skillpoint bundles only **counting once** for achievements — fixed.
- Skill points not being properly added to mana on a restart — fixed.
- Field tokens not persisting between sessions for the same slot — fixed.
- Already-completed stages not getting their visual tint when loading a new slot — fixed.
- Goal count differing between the apworld and the mod — fixed; the apworld is now the single source of truth.
- Generation failures and slow generations — much faster and more reliable now.
- W-tile getting unlocked when picking an S-stage starter — fixed.
- Drop icons not reloading properly when restarting a level — fixed.
- Drop icons showing one item at a time instead of all together — fixed.
- Achievement panel pips remaining in the previous stage's state between levels — fixed.
- "In Logic" button count differing from the in-game Achievement window count — fixed.
- The "5 skills available" achievement check incorrectly going out of logic — fixed.
- Field-token unlock items being incorrectly handled when stage IDs contained `e<x>` or `s<x>` — fixed.
- Battle Traits as a group not being properly considered for logic — fixed.
- Talisman items losing their proper names on drop icons in some seeds — fixed.
- Shadow Core stashes losing their base drop count — fixed.
- Achievement window crashing in rare scenarios — fixed.
- Ritual fights spawning too early with shadows already at high HP — fixed.
- Various seeds finishing in sphere 0 due to placement issues — fixed.
- Standalone mode leaking Archipelago achievements into the run — fixed.
- Many other small crashes and out-of-logic edge cases.

## Known Issues ##
- Achievement logic sometimes to loose, making achievements in logic that are technically impossible to get.
- Universal Tracker support has been extensively tested but may stay result in mismatches.  If so, please notify Levisaxos
V0.0.5.title: Added Achievements and more

v.0.02-pre-alpha.description:
Fixed a bug where the yaml generation would not properly take into account previous requirements.
Made W1-W4 free from start (required due to fill algorythm).
Game no longer forces a play in W1
v.0.02-pre-alpha.title: v0.02-pre-alpha

v0.0.1-pre-alpha.description:
GemCraft: Frostborn Wrath — Archipelago Mod v0.1.0-pre-alpha
First pre-alpha release. Expect bugs, please report them using the ingame button (will link to github)!

What's in the multiworld
Field Tokens — stages no longer auto-unlock, you receive tokens from the multiworld
Skills — all 24 wizard skills shuffled in
Battle Traits — all 15 traits shuffled in
XP Items — Scrolls, Tomes and Grimoires grant bonus wizard levels when received
Wizard stashes are still in the game but cannot grant skills or traits — Archipelago handles that.

Checks
Each stage has a Journey and a Bonus check. Play in Journey mode to send them.
Endurance and Trial modes are optional — use them if you need more XP.

Goal
Beat A4 with all 24 skills collected.

Options
skill_placement — per_zone puts one skill in each zone (recommended), spread is fully random
death_link — you know what this does
A bunch of DeathLink tuning options (gem loss, wave surges, grace period, cooldown)
Known issues
Wizard level gates are not enforced yet — XP items are there but don't block anything for now
No tracker or hint support yet
v0.0.1-pre-alpha.title: V0.0.1 - Pre Alpha

v0.0.3-pre-alpha.description:
New Features: 
Added option to force field tokens into different worlds
Added talisman fragment and shadow core unlocks from wizard stashes
Added disconnect panel with reconnect button
Added earlier skill distribution option
Moved wizard stashes to journey mode only

Improvements:
Tier requirements are now a percentage of fields per tier, with a configurable option
Improved generation: added P5 requirements and better skill spreading across tiers
Improved XP and SP collection to count up properly in the result screen
Updated message log: messages are stored when leaving and restored on return
Improved toast system: item and system messages are now split
Added click-lock on the connect button
Code refactoring and better internal game structure

Bugfixes: 
Fixed bug where XP tomes were not granting wizard levels
Fixed bug where different_world option caused a generation error
Fixed XP bar mismatch
Fixed level popup not showing AP bonus levels
Fixed soft-logic for skills; improved skill category accuracy
Fixed port number display in connection messages

v0.0.4-alpha.description:
Added:
Added Swarm Queen (K4) as an alternative goal
Added field completion as a goal (by field count or percentage)
Added ingame logic tracker showing which fields are in logic
Added visual options (stage tinting based on logic state)
Added a logic button on the map that pans to fields when clicked
Added enforce logic option
Added wave manipulation options (start wave, wave count, enemy hp, armour & shield)
Added ingame changelog viewer and version display

Bugfixes:
Fixed bug where talisman fragment would be rewarded multiple times after reload
Fixed bug where restarting a level caused unlocked skills and traits to be lost
Fixed non-scrollable settings panel
Fixed generation error in solo worlds with own_world option
v0.0.4-alpha.title: v0.0.4-alpha

v0.0.5.1.description:
# 0.0.5.1 — Logic Hotfix

Four bugs squashed since 0.0.5 — three on the logic-tracker side, plus a sneaky one that was silently breaking the in-game update notification for any version tagged with a suffix or with a fourth digit.

## Bug fixes since 0.0.5

- **Progressive Field Tokens left newly-unlocked stages stuck "out of logic" until the next item arrived.** The tracker now refreshes the moment any item is received — no more waiting for a follow-up grant — fixed.
- **"Call N waves early" achievements (Short Tempered, Restless, Agitated) gated on total wave count, not callable waves.** Linked-wave pairs auto-spawn together and only count as one early call, so the logic now uses each stage's true callable-wave maximum. A 36-wave stage with 6 linked pairs correctly tops out at 29 callable, and achievements requiring more on that stage stay locked instead of teasing the impossible — fixed.
- **Apparition-requiring achievements stayed out-of-logic when Q1 and R6 weren't reachable — even when the Ritual Battle Trait was owned.** Ritual reliably spawns 2 Apparitions on any stage with more than 3 waves, so it now satisfies the Apparition reachability gate — fixed.
- **In-game update notification ignored any release tag with a suffix (e.g. `-alpha`) or a fourth version digit**, so the "new version available" badge never fired for several past releases. Future releases will now notify correctly regardless of tag style — fixed.

## Known limitations
- This release ships **silently** to anyone still on 0.0.5 — the update-notifier fix is *in* this build, so existing 0.0.5 installs won't see the badge for it. From 0.0.5.1 onward, all future-release notifications will work.

## Install
See the [Setup guide](https://github.com/Levisaxos/Archipelago_GemcraftFrostbornWrath/blob/main/Setup.md).
v0.0.5.1.title: v0.0.5.1 — Logic Hotfix

v0.0.5.2.description:
This release gets the in-game logic and the tracker back in agreement, gives you more freedom over goal length, and removes an option that wasn't behaving.

## What's new

### Goal settings
- The minimum number of fields a goal can ask you to clear is now much lower (~10%), so you can set up **shorter runs** if you want.

### Options
- Removed the **Enforce Logic** option — it wasn't working the way it was intended, so it's gone for now.

## Bug fixes since 0.0.5.1

- Several achievements (including **Stumbling** and **Double Splash**) showed up as "in logic" on the tracker but could never actually be completed in-game — fixed. This hit achievements tied to high-HP / high-armor monsters and to non-monster creatures; the tracker and the game now agree on all of them.
v0.0.5.2.title: v0.0.5.2 - Logic Parity

v0.0.5.3.description:
A small follow-up release: earning achievements during an Archipelago game no longer touches your Steam account, plus a fix for a noisy reload.

## What's new

### Steam achievements
- Earning a GemCraft: Frostborn Wrath achievement during an **Archipelago** game no longer unlocks it on your **Steam** account — your Steam achievement progress stays exactly as it was. Normal (non-Archipelago) play still earns Steam achievements as usual.

## Bug fixes since 0.0.5.2

- A handful of already-earned achievements would re-appear in the item toast every time you reloaded — fixed.
v0.0.5.3.title: 0.0.5.3 — Steam-Safe Achievements

v0.0.5.4.description:
A small but important fix for anyone using **Universal Tracker**. If your field tokens are set to land in other players' worlds (or only in your own), the tracker would crash the moment you tried to open it. This release fixes that. No gameplay or generation behaviour changes otherwise.

## Bug fixes since 0.0.5.3

- Universal Tracker no longer crashes on launch for slots using the **different world** or **own world** field-token placement options. The tracker rebuilds each slot as a solo world to compute logic, which tripped a "requires more than one player" check — that check now correctly applies only to real solo games — fixed.

## Help
With help from @Klaty
v0.0.5.4.title: 0.0.5.4 — Universal Tracker Hotfix

v0.0.5.5.description:
This lets you set much shorter goals, and adds a developer convenience for sending achievement checks. No save-breaking changes.

## Logic & generation
- **Lower goal minimums.** `fields_count` now accepts **12–122** (was 50–122) and `fields_percentage` now accepts **10–100** (was 40–100), so you can generate much shorter seeds.
- **Trial-only achievements no longer gate Journey logic.** Several achievements that can realistically only be earned in Trial mode are marked untrackable so the fill never depends on them.

## Quality of life
- **Skill tooltip cleanup.** The Component-skill tooltip no longer says "*if the field has been beaten at least once*" — the randomizer removes that requirement, so the gem type unlocks regardless of whether you've cleared the field.

## Developer / debug
- New **Achievements** tab in the debug menu: send an achievement's location check without actually unlocking the achievement in-game. Lists the trackable, still-missing achievements for the connected slot.
v0.0.5.5.title: 0.0.5.5 — Shorter goals & QoL fix

v0.0.5.6.description:
This release makes switching back to a normal game after an Archipelago run completely clean — none of the randomizer's changes linger anymore — and adds an in-game credits screen. **Mod-only update:** the apworld is unchanged (still v0.0.5.5), so you only need to update the mod.

## What's new

### Credits screen
- Added an in-game **credits** screen, reachable from the main menu.

## Bug fixes since 0.0.5.5

Loading a regular (non-Archipelago) save after playing an Archipelago run used to leave pieces of the randomizer behind. Leaving Archipelago mode now resets all of it:
- The **Trial** and **Endurance** buttons could stay locked in a normal game — fixed.
- **Wizard stashes** stayed relocated to Journey mode instead of returning to Endurance — fixed.
- The **achievements screen** kept its Archipelago filtering, search box, and coloured dots, and hid the normal Reset button — fixed.
- The pause menu's **Return to map** / **Restart** buttons and the victory screen's restart button behaved as they do inside an Archipelago run — fixed.

## Known limitations
- Still an alpha — expect the occasional logic bug.
v0.0.5.6.title: 0.0.5.6 — Standalone Cleanup
